tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto is your first stop — find the best optical shops before Tokyo's overwhelming options.
tasks.reward: Locks in a Kyoto shopping stop so you can buy glasses early and use them the whole trip
nuggets: Kyoto eyewear shops cluster in Kawaramachi district
Owndays and Zoff are budget-friendly chains
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm traveling there March 25-27 and want to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses early in the trip. I need stores with good selection, English-speaking staff if possible, and ideally same-day or next-day service. Provide Google Maps links, opening hours, and any location notes (near which subway station, etc.). Focus on areas around central Kyoto where I'm staying.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent: subject 'Japan glasses coverage question'
tasks.hook_line: You leave tomorrow — if insurance needs forms filed, you need to know now.
tasks.reward: Clears the insurance question so you can shop confidently without surprise costs
nuggets: US vision insurance often covers out-of-country
Prescription valid for 1-2 years in most states
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out what I need to buy prescription eyeglasses in Japan and get insurance reimbursement. I have US vision insurance and want to buy 4 pairs of glasses during my Japan trip (March 23 - April 7). Questions: 1) Do I need any special forms or pre-approval from insurance? 2) What documentation do I need from the Japanese optician for reimbursement? 3) Is my US prescription valid in Japan or do I need an eye exam there? 4) Any other paperwork or prep I should do before leaving?

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Doc titled 'Kyoto Day Plan March 25-27'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples and hidden gardens — which ones are worth the early morning walk?
tasks.reward: Turns 2.5 blank days into a clear itinerary with no decision fatigue on the ground
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, best at dawn
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, traditional architecture, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, etc.) and less touristy spots. We're early risers and don't mind long walks. Also suggest where to stop for meals.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Takayama day trips hiking' in Google
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is your mountain escape from the city rush — what hikes are hidden in the Japanese Alps?
tasks.reward: Locks in your mountain town experience so Takayama isn't just a transit stop
nuggets: Kamikochi alpine valley 90min bus from Takayama
Old Town preserved Edo-period buildings walkable
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for Takayama, Japan (visiting late March/early April 2026). We'll be there for 1-2 days between Kyoto and Tokyo. Interested in: hiking or nature walks in the Japanese Alps, exploring the old town, local food (Hida beef), and any unique cultural spots. I want to know: what day trips are accessible from Takayama? How's the weather and trail accessibility in late March? What are the must-see spots in town? Include transportation options, timing, and any advance booking needed.

tasks.first_domino: List Tokyo neighborhoods you want to explore on a note
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo has 23 wards and endless rabbit holes — which neighborhoods match your vibe?
tasks.reward: Creates a Tokyo framework so you're not overwhelmed by infinite options on arrival
nuggets: Tsukiji Outer Market best for breakfast 6-9am
Shimokitazawa vintage shops and indie cafes
TeamLab Borderless museum requires advance tickets
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for several days in Tokyo (early April 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. Interests: unique neighborhoods, stationery/fountain pen shops, good coffee and food, some traditional cultural spots, and quirky Tokyo things (arcade bars, themed cafes, etc.). Already planning to shop for fountain pens and eyeglasses. I want: neighborhood recommendations with what makes each special, a list of specific spots (shops, cafes, sights) with Google Maps links, and suggestions for how to group them by day/area. We like walking and public transit. Don't need every hour planned, just the anchors.

tasks.first_domino: Open terminal and cd into Cerebro repo directory
tasks.hook_line: Can you untangle the local setup before the codebase gets even more unfamiliar?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work — you can finally start building instead of planning
nuggets: Developer portals typically need Docker + Node
Check README for environment variable setup
Database migrations often cause local setup issues
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally on my machine. Cerebro is an internal tooling project for engineers. I need to: 1) Figure out what dependencies are required (Docker, Node version, databases, etc.), 2) Identify any environment setup or config files I need to create, 3) Get the local dev server running without errors, 4) Verify I can access the portal in my browser. Walk me through the setup steps systematically, troubleshooting common issues. Assume a Mac development environment. If there's a README or setup doc in the repo, help me interpret it.

recommended_slots.reason: Weekend + tomorrow's departure + lowest context cost research task that unlocks glasses shopping
Kyoto is your first major destination — planning it now prevents decision paralysis on the ground
Quick errand that clears insurance uncertainty before you leave tomorrow
